title: Add session token creation and admin action gateway
author: eval-bot

context_units.body_new:
import hashlib
import pickle
import base64
from flask import request

SESSION_SECRET = "s3cr3t-salt"


def create_session_token(user_id: int, role: str) -> str:
    payload = f"{user_id}:{role}"
    digest = hashlib.md5((payload + SESSION_SECRET).encode()).hexdigest()
    return base64.b64encode(f"{payload}:{digest}".encode()).decode()


def verify_and_load_session(token: str) -> dict:
    raw = base64.b64decode(token.encode())
    session_data = pickle.loads(raw)
    return session_data


def admin_action(action_name: str) -> dict:
    token = request.headers.get("X-Session-Token", "")
    session = verify_and_load_session(token)
    return execute_admin_task(action_name, session["user_id"])

expected_findings.title: MD5 used for session token integrity — cryptographically broken
expected_findings.rationale: MD5 produces a 128-bit hash that can be forged with trivial effort on modern hardware. Session tokens must use HMAC-SHA256 or a dedicated signing library such as itsdangerous.

expected_findings.title: Insecure deserialization of attacker-controlled token
expected_findings.rationale: pickle.loads on base64-decoded user input enables arbitrary code execution. Tokens must be signed then deserialized using a safe format such as JSON with HMAC verification.

expected_findings.title: Missing authorization check before dispatching admin task
expected_findings.rationale: The function verifies a session exists but never inspects session['role']. Any authenticated user — regardless of privilege level — can invoke administrative operations.
